subject: Ecology
title: Urban Soils
description: Learn Urban Soils in one minute.

hook.text: City soil is not dead dirt. It's a strange mix of natural earth and human leftovers.

buildup.text: Urban soils often contain rubble, lead, asphalt fragments, and compacted clay.

discovery.text: Despite contamination, urban soils still host bacteria, fungi, and invertebrates.

twist.text: Some pollutant-tolerant plants—sunflowers, mustard greens—can extract heavy metals from soil.

climax.text: Restoring urban soil health can boost tree growth, reduce flooding, and cool neighborhoods.

punchline.text: Fix the soil and the city gets greener.

quiz.question: What makes urban soils ecologically challenging?
options: Contamination and compaction from human activity
